body:
@sepo-agent /answer 

- For example, if we tag the agent for reviewing in the chat, we should display as labels 
- the same should be true for orchestrator; but we should not remove the orchestrator tag until it finishes 

Can you plan for it?

comments.body:
I checked issue #25 and related context. There’s no parent/tracker linked from #25, but related issue [#19](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/19) exists and its implementation PR [#20](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/20) is still open (not merged), so `main` does not yet include explicit `/orchestrate` routing.

Current behavior in `main`:
- Mention runs are routed in [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml) and only apply the fixed `agent` status label via [`add-label.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/add-label.ts).
- Label-triggered runs are handled by [`agent-label.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml), which removes the triggering `agent/*` label after router success.
- Orchestrator handoff is dispatched from [`dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/dispatch-agent-orchestrator.ts) into [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml), with no dedicated orchestrator activity label lifecycle.

Proposed implementation approach:
1. Add route-activity label management (separate from fixed `agent` status label), with explicit add/remove operations in CLI helpers.
2. Apply corresponding activity label when a route starts (review, fix-pr, implement, orchestrator path).
3. Remove activity labels in terminal workflow steps (`if: always()`), so cleanup happens even on failures.
4. Special-case orchestrator: keep its label until orchestrator workflow actually completes; do not let generic trigger-label cleanup remove it early.
5. Keep trigger-label semantics (`agent/*`) and status/activity semantics separate to avoid accidental retriggers and cleanup races.

Execution plan (for approval):
1. Extend GitHub label helpers in [`github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts) with remove-label support.
2. Add a new CLI for route activity labels (add/remove by route + target kind/number), and tests under [`.agent/src/__tests__`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/main/.agent/src/__tests__).
3. Wire label-add at route start in [`agent-router.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml) and workflow entry points where needed.
4. Wire label-remove at route completion in [`agent-review.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-review.yml), [`agent-fix-pr.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-fix-pr.yml), [`agent-implement.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml), and [`agent-orchestrator.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml).
5. Update [`agent-label.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml) cleanup logic so orchestrator-tag cleanup is deferred until orchestrator completion.
6. Update assertions in [`envelope.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/__tests__/envelope.test.ts) and docs in [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md).

Please confirm and I’ll start implementation.

Clarifications to unblock cleanly:
1. Should “corresponding label” reuse trigger names (`agent/review`, `agent/orchestrate`) or use separate non-trigger activity labels (recommended)?
2. For orchestrator, should label removal happen when `agent-orchestrator.yml` ends, or only after the full multi-step automation chain reaches stop?

@sepo-agent /answer 

> For orchestrator, should label removal happen when agent-orchestrator.yml ends, or only after the full multi-step automation chain reaches stop?

OK actually we've added a lot of features for the agent orchestrator -- can you review the new features for orchestrator? And to answer you question, yeah we should remove after multi-step automation stops.

> Should “corresponding label” reuse trigger names (agent/review, agent/orchestrate) or use separate non-trigger activity labels (recommended)?

Yeah that is a great question. If we use a different tag, what type should that be?
And if we reuse the trigger names, how messy the code is going to be?

I reviewed the current orchestrator feature set on `main` and ran the focused test subset.

Findings:

1. High: reusing `agent/orchestrate` as a running label conflicts with current cleanup.
[agent-label.yml](/Users/shannon/projects/2604_sepo/repo/.agent/tools/local-runner/runner-1/_work/repo/repo/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml:48) removes every accepted `agent/*` label immediately after `agent-router.yml` succeeds. For `/orchestrate`, router success only means “orchestrator workflow dispatched” ([agent-router.yml](/Users/shannon/projects/2604_sepo/repo/.agent/tools/local-runner/runner-1/_work/repo/repo/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml:682)); the multi-step chain has not finished. So `agent/orchestrate` cannot currently represent “orchestration is running”.

2. Medium: terminal orchestrator UX is uneven.
[orchestrate-handoff.ts](/Users/shannon/projects/2604_sepo/repo/.agent/tools/local-runner/runner-1/_work/repo/repo/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts:1157) handles terminal decisions, but visible stop comments are only emitted for selected cases. A normal chain ending after a review `SHIP` can stop without a final “orchestration complete” comment. That is relevant if we also remove a running label at terminal stop, because the label may disappear without a clear final orchestration marker.

3. No blocker found in core route/policy logic.
The explicit `/orchestrate` route, planner validation, sub-issue delegation, base branch/PR propagation, and `orchestration_enabled` handoff gating are well covered. I ran:
`npm --prefix .agent run build`
`node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/handoff.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrator-preflight-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/implementation-base.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/sub-orchestration.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`

Result: `170` tests passing.

For the label design: I recommend separate non-trigger activity labels.

Use labels like:
- `sepo/running-review`
- `sepo/running-fix-pr`
- `sepo/running-implement`
- `sepo/running-orchestrate`

Reason: anything under `agent/` is currently a trigger label because [agent-label.yml](/Users/shannon/projects/2604_sepo/repo/.agent/tools/local-runner/runner-1/_work/repo/repo/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml:32) starts workflows for `startsWith(label, 'agent/')`. If we reuse trigger names, the same label has two meanings: “please run this” and “this is running”. That makes cleanup, reruns, and retriggers much more fragile.

How messy reuse would be:
- For `agent/review`, `agent/fix-pr`, `agent/implement`, we would need to skip `agent-label.yml` cleanup and move cleanup into every terminal workflow.
- For `agent/orchestrate`, cleanup must move into terminal orchestrator logic after the whole chain stops, not after router dispatch.
- Re-adding labels from workflows can accidentally trigger `agent-label.yml` unless every path is carefully guarded.
- Users cannot rerun by applying the same label while it remains present.

So the cleaner model is:
- `agent/*` means trigger.
- `agent` means handled status.
- `sepo/running-*` means current activity/status.

For your answer: orchestrator running label should be removed only when the multi-step automation stops, not when `agent-orchestrator.yml` merely dispatches the next child workflow.

> [agent-label.yml](https://github.com/Users/shannon/projects/2604_sepo/repo/.agent/tools/local-runner/runner-1/_work/repo/repo/.github/workflows/agent-label.yml:48) removes every accepted agent/* label immediately after agent-router.yml succeeds.

Yeah I agree. But how about we can composite this: 
- Add a separate label called agent-running  
- If agent-running is included, then we should keep the labels 
- And then after agent-running is removed, then we remove the agent/* labels? 

@sepo-agent /answer 

But that might not lead to a clean implementation?

I’ll answer this as a design review of the label lifecycle, using the current workflow shape: `agent/*` labels are trigger labels, and any running-state label should avoid becoming another trigger path.I would not implement it as “`agent-running` controls whether we keep `agent/*` labels.”

It can work, but it couples two separate concepts:

- `agent/*`: queue/trigger labels
- `agent-running`: execution state

That coupling creates edge cases:
- If `agent-running` removal fails, stale `agent/*` labels remain and users cannot re-apply the same trigger label cleanly.
- If someone manually removes `agent-running`, we need a second cleanup workflow to remove `agent/*`, which itself must avoid racing active jobs.
- Multiple route labels become ambiguous: `agent/review` plus `agent/fix-pr` plus `agent-running` does not say which action is actually running.
- For orchestrator, keeping `agent/orchestrate` around until the chain stops means the trigger label doubles as a status label, which makes retries and manual reruns less obvious.

Cleaner implementation:

- Keep `agent/*` as one-shot trigger labels.
- Add separate activity labels that never trigger workflows:
  - `agent-running` for generic “Sepo is active”
  - optionally `agent-running/review`, `agent-running/fix-pr`, `agent-running/implement`, `agent-running/orchestrate` if we want route visibility
- Remove `agent/*` trigger labels immediately after the router accepts the request, as today.
- Add running labels when the real route starts.
- Remove route running labels when that route finishes.
- Remove `agent-running/orchestrate` only when the whole orchestration chain stops.

If you want the simplest useful version, I’d do just two labels first:

- `agent-running`
- `agent-running/orchestrate`

Then later add per-route labels if the generic one is not enough. Note: because `agent-label.yml` triggers on `startsWith(label, 'agent/')`, I would avoid names like `agent/running/*`. Use `agent-running` or `sepo/running-*` so adding the status label does not create extra workflow runs.

> Add separate activity labels that never trigger workflows:
> agent-running for generic “Sepo is active”
> optionally agent-running/review, agent-running/fix-pr, agent-running/implement, agent-running/orchestrate if we want route visibility

Sure kinda makes sense; route visibility should be ideal? 

> Remove agent/* trigger labels immediately after the router accepts the request, as today.
> Add running labels when the real route starts.

is this completely true? for example, when we run agent/review, it will only remove the labels when it finishes?

@sepo-agent /answer

Not completely true if “labels” means `agent/review`.

The cleaner lifecycle is:

1. User applies `agent/review`.
2. `agent-label.yml` triggers.
3. Router accepts the request.
4. Remove `agent/review` immediately, because it is a trigger/queue label.
5. Add `agent-running/review` when the actual review workflow starts.
6. Remove `agent-running/review` when review finishes.

So the visible in-progress label would be `agent-running/review`, not `agent/review`.

For orchestrator:

1. User applies `agent/orchestrate`.
2. Remove `agent/orchestrate` after router accepts it.
3. Add `agent-running/orchestrate`.
4. Keep `agent-running/orchestrate` across implement/review/fix-pr handoffs.
5. Remove `agent-running/orchestrate` only when the orchestration chain reaches a terminal stop.

Route visibility is ideal, but it should be represented by non-trigger activity labels. Reusing `agent/review` as both “please run review” and “review is running” is where the implementation gets messy.

title: Display the corresponding label when running the action
